Update `lenses/RBSA-SpecTop.adoc` to add RBRE, RBRG, RBRS regime definitions and normalize RBRO annotation.

**Read the file first**, then apply ALL edits using the Edit tool. Be very precise with old_string matching.

## Edit 1: Add RBRE mapping entries

After the RBRO mapping block (around line 280), insert RBRE mappings. Current:
```
:rbro_regime:                <<rbro_regime,RBRO Regime>>

// Recipe Bottle Regime: Vessel (container image specifications)
```

Change to:
```
:rbro_regime:                <<rbro_regime,RBRO Regime>>

// Recipe Bottle Regime: ECR (AWS container registry)
:rbre_aws_access_key_id:     <<rbre_aws_access_key_id,RBRE_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID>>
:rbre_aws_account_id:        <<rbre_aws_account_id,RBRE_AWS_ACCOUNT_ID>>
:rbre_aws_credentials_env:   <<rbre_aws_credentials_env,RBRE_AWS_CREDENTIALS_ENV>>
:rbre_aws_region:            <<rbre_aws_region,RBRE_AWS_REGION>>
:rbre_aws_secret_access_key: <<rbre_aws_secret_access_key,RBRE_AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY>>
:rbre_regime:                <<rbre_regime,RBRE Regime>>
:rbre_repository_name:       <<rbre_repository_name,RBRE_REPOSITORY_NAME>>

// Recipe Bottle Regime: GitHub (GitHub authentication)
:rbrg_pat:                   <<rbrg_pat,RBRG_PAT>>
:rbrg_regime:                <<rbrg_regime,RBRG Regime>>
:rbrg_username:              <<rbrg_username,RBRG_USERNAME>>

// Recipe Bottle Regime: Station (workstation Podman configuration)
:rbrs_podman_root_dir:       <<rbrs_podman_root_dir,RBRS_PODMAN_ROOT_DIR>>
:rbrs_regime:                <<rbrs_regime,RBRS Regime>>
:rbrs_vm_platform:           <<rbrs_vm_platform,RBRS_VM_PLATFORM>>
:rbrs_vmimage_cache_dir:     <<rbrs_vmimage_cache_dir,RBRS_VMIMAGE_CACHE_DIR>>

// Recipe Bottle Regime: Vessel (container image specifications)
```

In the prefix section (around lines 307-313), current:
```

:rbra_prefix:                <<rbra_prefix,RBRA_>>
:rbro_prefix:                <<rbro_prefix,RBRO_>>
```

```
:rbra_prefix:                <<rbra_prefix,RBRA_>>
```

## Edit 3: Add prefix definitions

Find the area where prefixes are defined (around lines 1620-1640). The existing pattern looks like:

```
[[rbro_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{rbro_prefix}::
Recipe Bottle OAuth Regime
```

After the existing prefix definitions block, add definitions for rbre, rbrg, rbrs. They should be inserted alphabetically among the existing definitions. Look for the prefix definition block and add:

```
[[rbre_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{rbre_prefix}::
Recipe Bottle ECR Regime

[[rbrg_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{rbrg_prefix}::
Recipe Bottle GitHub Regime

[[rbrs_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{rbrs_prefix}::
Recipe Bottle Station Regime
```

Insert these in alphabetical position among existing prefix defs.

## Edit 4: Normalize RBRO annotation

At the `[[rbro_regime]]` definition (around line 1998-1999), current:
```
[[rbro_regime]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_regime axf_bash axrd_singleton⟧
```

Change annotation to:
```
[[rbro_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
```

## Edit 5: Add 3 new regime sections

After the RBRO section (which ends around line 2016 with the RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN definition), and BEFORE the "=== Kit Regime Operations (RBK)" section (around line 2018), insert 3 new sections:

```
=== ECR Regime (RBRE)

The ECR regime defines AWS credentials and repository configuration for pushing and pulling container images via Amazon Elastic Container Registry.

[[rbre_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
{rbre_regime}::
An
regime
that contains AWS credentials and ECR repository configuration for container image operations via Amazon Elastic Container Registry.

[[rbre_aws_credentials_env]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbre_aws_credentials_env}::
AWS credentials environment name identifying the credential set.

[[rbre_aws_access_key_id]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbre_aws_access_key_id}::
AWS access key identifier for programmatic access to ECR services.

[[rbre_aws_secret_access_key]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbre_aws_secret_access_key}::
AWS secret access key for signing API requests.
Security-sensitive; masked in render output.

[[rbre_aws_account_id]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbre_aws_account_id}::
AWS account identifier owning the ECR registry.

[[rbre_aws_region]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbre_aws_region}::
AWS region where the ECR registry is hosted.

[[rbre_repository_name]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname⟧
{rbre_repository_name}::
ECR repository name for container image storage.

include::RBSRE-RegimeEcr.adoc[]

=== GitHub Regime (RBRG)

The GitHub regime defines authentication credentials for GitHub repository operations and container registry access.

[[rbrg_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
{rbrg_regime}::
An
regime
that contains GitHub authentication credentials for repository operations and container registry access.

[[rbrg_username]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname⟧
{rbrg_username}::
GitHub username for repository and registry authentication.

[[rbrg_pat]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbrg_pat}::
GitHub Personal Access Token for API and registry authentication.
Security-sensitive; masked in render output, never logged.

include::RBSRG-RegimeGithub.adoc[]

=== Station Regime (RBRS)

The station regime defines workstation-specific Podman VM configuration for the local development environment.

[[rbrs_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
{rbrs_regime}::
An
regime
that contains workstation-specific Podman VM configuration for the local development environment.

[[rbrs_podman_root_dir]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbrs_podman_root_dir}::
Root directory for Podman machine storage on the local workstation.

[[rbrs_vmimage_cache_dir]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbrs_vmimage_cache_dir}::
Directory for caching VM images used by Podman machine initialization.

[[rbrs_vm_platform]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{rbrs_vm_platform}::
Platform architecture identifier for the Podman VM.

include::RBSRS-RegimeStation.adoc[]

```

This block should be inserted between the last RBRO content and the `=== Kit Regime Operations (RBK)` header.

## Summary of all edits
1. Add mapping entries for RBRE (7 entries), RBRG (3 entries), RBRS (4 entries)
2. Add prefix entries (rbre, rbrg, rbrs) alphabetically
3. Add prefix definitions (rbre_prefix, rbrg_prefix, rbrs_prefix)
4. Normalize RBRO annotation: `axl_voices axrg_regime` → `axvr_regime` + add `axrd_file_sourced`
5. Add 3 new `===` sections with variable definitions and includes

   416→// ============================================================================
   417→// TIER 1: WHY AND WHAT
   418→// ============================================================================
   419→
   420→== System Overview
   421→
   422→=== Core Trust Challenge
   423→
   424→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   425→{st_container}-based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   426→
   427→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   428→
   429→*
   430→{st_container_s}
   431→provide powerful tool isolation
   432→* But they need controlled access to do useful work
   433→* Standard
   434→{st_container}
   435→networking grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   436→
   437→=== The Bottle Pattern
   438→
   439→{at_rbm_system}
   440→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   441→{st_container}
   442→type here referred to as a
   443→{at_bottle_container}:
   444→
   445→. File Access Boundary
   446→*
   447→{at_bottle_container}
   448→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   449→{st_volume_mount_s}
   450→* No other access to
   451→{at_workstation}
   452→filesystem
   453→
   454→. Network Boundary
   455→*
   456→{at_bottle_container}
   457→has no direct network access
   458→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   459→{at_sentry_container},
   460→a
   461→{st_container}
   462→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   463→* Only declared
   464→{st_port_map_s}
   465→and destinations allowed
   466→* Full logging of allowed and denied traffic
   467→
   468→Each
   469→{at_bottle_service}
   470→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   471→
   472→* One
   473→{at_sentry_container}
   474→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   475→* One
   476→{at_censer_container}
   477→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   478→* One
   479→{at_bottle_container}
   480→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   481→* Clear configuration in a single
   482→{at_nameplate}
   483→file
   484→
   485→The
   486→{at_censer_container}
   487→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   488→{at_sentry_container},
   489→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   490→{at_bottle_container}
   491→via `--net=container:censer`.
   492→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   493→{at_bottle_container}
   494→experiences only a functional path to its
   495→{at_sentry_container}
   496→gateway
   497→
   498→=== Security Properties
   499→
   500→The
